canonical_question: What are the employment law rules for hiring remote workers internationally?
aliases: Cross-border employment law for distributed teams
Remote work tax and legal obligations by jurisdiction
constraints: Jurisdiction-specific rules vary by country and bilateral treaty; this unit covers the global framework and major jurisdictions (US, EU, UK) but cannot replace local legal counsel
OECD 2025 Model Tax Convention update (Nov 2025) is non-binding commentary; may not yet be reflected in all bilateral treaties
EU Framework Agreement on cross-border telework (July 2023) applies only to signatory EU/EEA states plus Switzerland; UK is not a signatory
US totalization agreements cover only 30 bilateral partners; dual social security taxation risk exists for all other countries
Local employment law overrides contractual choice-of-law clauses in most jurisdictions
Misclassification penalties and enforcement vary dramatically by jurisdiction; amounts cited are representative, not exhaustive

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User is asking about data privacy compliance for remote workers rather than employment/tax law
User needs specific GDPR or CCPA data handling rules for distributed teams
Worker is a genuinely independent contractor and user needs classification guidance

related_to.label: GDPR compliance requirements (data privacy obligations for remote teams)
Cross-border data transfer rules for distributed workforces

inputs_needed.question: Where is the employer's legal entity incorporated?
Where will the remote worker physically perform work?
Is the worker an employee or independent contractor?
What percentage of working time will the worker spend in the foreign country?
How long will the remote work arrangement last?

applies_to.user_segment: Employers hiring remote workers across international borders or US state lines
applies_to.context: Companies with distributed workforces, digital nomads, or cross-border contractor arrangements needing to manage PE risk, social security, employment law, payroll tax, and data privacy
